---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.04
    center: false
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    slide-number: false
    progress: true
    controls: true
    controls-layout: edges
    hash: true
    css: styles.css
---

## {.title}

<div class="cover-wrap">
  <div class="cover-title"><span class="flow">Cognitive Biases &amp; Heuristics</span></div>
  <div class="cover-rule"></div>
  <div class="cover-author">Anissa Chan</div>
</div>

## Anchoring Effect

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="52%"}
<div class="image-card tall-card">
  <img src="assets/anchoring-cars.png" alt="Anchoring effect illustration">
</div>
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
<div class="content-block">
  <div class="lead-label">What is it?</div>
  <ul>
    <li>Overreliance on first information seen.</li>
  </ul>

  <div class="analysis-label">Seen in Data Analysis</div>
  <ul>
    <li>Updated information is neglected</li>
  </ul>
</div>
:::
::::

## Confirmation Bias

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="45%"}
<div class="content-block">
  <div class="lead-label">What is it?</div>
  <ul>
    <li>Tendency to ignore information that does not affirm preset beliefs</li>
  </ul>

  <div class="analysis-label">Seen in Data Analysis</div>
  <ul>
    <li>Experiments are ran to confirm beliefs</li>
    <li>New ideas passed over</li>
  </ul>
</div>
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}
<div class="visual-panel confirmation-panel">
  <div class="image-card confirmation-base">
    <img src="assets/confirmation-venn.png" alt="Confirmation bias venn diagram">
  </div>

  <div class="image-card confirmation-overlay blueberries-overlay fragment fade-in" data-fragment-index="1">
    <img src="assets/confirmation-blueberries.jpeg" alt="Blueberries photo">
  </div>

  <div class="image-card confirmation-overlay typing-overlay fragment fade-in" data-fragment-index="2">
    <img src="assets/confirmation-typing.gif" alt="Typing gif">
  </div>
</div>
:::
::::

## Availability heuristic

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="52%"}
<div class="visual-panel availability-panel">
  <div class="image-card availability-base">
    <img src="assets/availability-cartoon.png" alt="Availability heuristic illustration">
  </div>

  <div class="image-card availability-overlay fragment fade-in" data-fragment-index="1">
    <img src="assets/availability-table.png" alt="Availability heuristic table">
  </div>
</div>
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
<div class="content-block">
  <div class="lead-label">What is it?</div>
  <ul>
    <li>Tendency to rely on memorable ideas and events</li>
  </ul>

  <div class="analysis-label">Seen in Data Analysis</div>
  <ul>
    <li>Overemphasize information that is more memorable</li>
    <li>Ignoring information that is less memorable</li>
  </ul>
</div>
:::
::::

## Conclusion {.band-slide .conclusion-slide}

<div class="bottom-band"><img src="assets/bottom-photo.jpg" alt="Bottom photo"></div>

## Sources {.band-slide .sources-slide}

<div class="sources-wrap">
  <p>https://www.floridamuseum.ufl.edu/shark-attacks/yearly-worldwide-summary/</p>
  <p>https://www.pon.harvard.edu/tag/anchoring-effect/</p>
  <p>https://lpsonline.sas.upenn.edu/features/cognitive-bias-and-data-how-human-psychology-impacts-data-interpretatio</p>
  <p>https://oxford-review.com/the-oxford-review-dei-diversity-equity-and-inclusion-dictionary/confirmation-bias-definition-and-explanation/</p>
  <p>n</p>
</div>
<div class="bottom-band"><img src="assets/bottom-photo.jpg" alt="Bottom photo"></div>
